2605.12294.title: Executable Agentic Memory for GUI Agent
2605.12294.paper_summary: Modern GUI agents typically rely on a model-centric and step-wise interaction paradigm, where LLMs must re-interpret the UI and re-decide actions at every screen, which is fragile in long-horizon tasks. In this paper, we propose Executable Agentic Memory (EAM), a structured Knowledge Graph (KG) that shifts GUI planning from free-form generation to a robust retrieval-and-execution process. Our approach includes a sample-efficient memory construction pipeline using state-aware DFS and action-group mining to compress multi-step routines. To ensure efficient planning, we introduce a value-guided graph search where a lightweight Q-function model steers Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) over the KG. We theoretically establish bias-consistency for the Q-model and derive sample complexity bounds for path recovery. Empirically, EAM outperforms state-of-the-art baselines like UI-TARS-7B by up to $19.6\%$ on AndroidWorld, while reducing token costs $6\times$ relative to GPT-4o. With a $2.8$s average latency, EAM enables reliable, quick, and long-horizon GUI automation.
2605.12294.paper_summary_zh: 現代的 GUI 代理通常依賴於一種以模型為中心的逐步交互範式，其中 LLM 必須在每個屏幕上重新解釋 UI 並重新決定行動，這在長期任務中是脆弱的。在本文中，我們提出了可執行的代理記憶（EAM），這是一種結構化的知識圖譜（KG），將 GUI 規劃從自由形式生成轉變為穩健的檢索與執行過程。我們的方法包括一個樣本高效的記憶構建管道，使用狀態感知的深度優先搜索（DFS）和行動群組挖掘來壓縮多步例程。為了確保高效的規劃，我們引入了一種價值引導的圖搜索，其中輕量級的 Q 函數模型引導蒙特卡羅樹搜索（MCTS）在 KG 上進行。我們在理論上建立了 Q 模型的偏差一致性，並推導了路徑恢復的樣本複雜度界限。在實證上，EAM 在 AndroidWorld 上的表現超過了最先進的基準，如 UI-TARS-7B，提升幅度達到 $19.6\%$，同時將相對於 GPT-4o 的標記成本降低了 $6\times$。EAM 以 $2.8$s 的平均延遲，使得可靠、快速且長期的 GUI 自動化成為可能。
2605.12294.author: Zerui Qin et.al.
2605.12294.authors: Zerui Qin, Sheng Yue, Xingyuan Hua, Yongjian Fu, Ju Ren
